claim: Expert-rubric judges showed higher reported inter-judge agreement than generic-rubric judges.
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: Supports testing skill-grounded scoring as a reliability intervention, but not immediate adoption.

claim: Generic-rubric judges had lower reported inter-judge agreement.
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: Provides the comparison condition while limiting causal attribution.

claim: Expert-rubric judges showed lower reported judge variance.
decision_use: Supports a reliability signal, not a validity conclusion.

claim: Generic-rubric judges showed higher reported judge variance.
decision_use: Provides disconfirmable comparative context and reinforces the validity boundary.

claim: Automated output grades had weak concordance with human judgments at the individual-run level.
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: Argues against production adoption until human criterion validity is demonstrated.

claim: Automated skill grades had weak concordance with human judgments at the individual-run level.
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: Requires independent process annotation in a controlled pilot.

claim: The skill-execution ablation was small and effects were heterogeneous.
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: Precludes a stable causal adoption claim and motivates randomization and factor controls.

claim: One configured system showed a large directional skill gain.
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: Supports equipoise for a pilot but not a rollout decision.

claim: Only a minority of no-skill runs deployed in the paired ablation.
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: Motivates deployment as an outcome while requiring controls for integration and stochasticity.

claim: A majority of pairwise-winning outputs still did not necessarily establish universal professional readiness.
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: Requires an absolute readiness gate with multiple experts before adoption.
